doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_set_divby0 (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L216-L222). One-line C body: `__gmpfr_flags |= MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0` -- set the DIVBY0 bit (bit 5 = 32) in the global flag register. This is the OR-set mirror of mpfr_clear_divby0's `&= ALL ^ BIT`. C returns void.

The TS port follows the same immutable-API contract used by the rest of the flag family (see mpfr_clear_divby0, mpfr_divby0_p): it takes a `mask: bigint` describing the pre-set flag state, applies the set-DIVBY0 operation, and returns the new flag state as a `bigint`. This makes the port pure/stateless on its surface (the wire form encodes the global flag state explicitly), while still exercising the SAME `flags |= BIT` arithmetic the C performs.

The in-library wiring (when ported into src/) routes through setFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0) from src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts. The reference port reads that delegate; the production src/ops/set_divby0.ts will too.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- the input domain (mask in [0, 63]) is small but enumerable. happy/edge/adversarial/fuzz coverage is built on the same six-flag truth-table the mpfr_clear_divby0 golden uses, so the same coverage shape applies.
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C returns void (mutates global state); TS returns `bigint` -- the new flag state after the set operation. The immutable-API mandate (decision_api_shape memory) forbids global mutable state in the public surface.

  2. Parameter list: C takes no parameters (operates on the global __gmpfr_flags register); TS takes `mask: bigint` -- the desired pre-set flag state. Matches the convention used by mpfr_clear_divby0 and the rest of the flag family.

  3. Mechanism: The port must either (a) use src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts directly (setFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0)) or (b) compute the result inline as `(mask | MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0) & MPFR_FLAGS_ALL` = `(mask | 32n) & 63n`. Both are equivalent. Option (a) ties the port into the shared substrate; option (b) is self-contained. The reference port uses option (a) for consistency with the rest of the flag family.
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L216-L222 -- the C reference body (`__gmpfr_flags |= MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0`).
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- the TS flag register module (already shipped); setFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_DIVBY0) is the canonical set path.
src/ops/clear_divby0.ts -- sister clear op that touches the same bit; uses the same mask-based wire form.
mpfr/tests/texceptions.c L327-L351 -- mined source pattern (clear, set_X, verify).
